references.excerpt_text: Advised in Jvara (fever) and Kasa (cough), particularly suitable for Pinasa (common cold/URTI) presentations.

explanation.summary: Vyaghryadi Kashayam is a decoction of Vyaghri (Kantakari), Shunthi, and Pippali — herbs with Ushna (hot) and Katu (pungent) properties that specifically address Pinasa (common cold) with its associated Kapha vitiation and nasal symptoms. It directly matches Pinasa as a primary indication, unlike Punarnavadi which addresses systemic inflammatory edema (Shotha).

claims.text: Vyaghryadi has Pinasa/Pratishyaya as a primary indication, directly matching URTI presentation.
Kantakari (Vyaghri) and Shunthi possess Ushna Virya that resolves Kapha accumulation in the upper respiratory tract.
Punarnavadi Kashayam, while also indicated in Jvara and Kasa, primarily targets Shotha (inflammatory edema) — not present in this case.

results.differentiation_note: Vyaghryadi is hot and dry, making it better suited than Punarnavadi for patients with common cold (Pinasa) and runny nose, whereas Punarnavadi is appropriate for generalized inflammatory swelling (Shotha).

references.excerpt_text: Diuretic and anti-inflammatory. Advised in Fever (Jvara), Cough (Kasa), generalized inflammatory changes/swelling (Shotha).

explanation.summary: Punarnavadi Kashayam is indicated in Jvara and Kasa, but its primary specialization is Shotha (edema/inflammation) — not present in this Pinasa (common cold/URTI) case. Without systemic inflammatory component, Punarnavadi is a lower-ranked choice here.

claims.text: Punarnavadi's primary indications are Shotha (edema) and Mutra Vikara (urinary disorders), not specifically Pinasa.

results.differentiation_note: Best suited when generalized inflammatory swelling (Shotha) is the dominant feature.

disclaimer: Educational decision support only. Not a diagnosis or prescription. Clinical judgment of a qualified vaidya is required.
